isolation.testingApproach: curl against public and internal endpoints

assertions.title: Public provider routes are not usable as raw browser inference proxies

steps.action: POST /provider/v1/inference from public origin with Mozilla User-Agent
POST /provider/v1/inference from public origin with curl User-Agent
POST /provider/v1/inference with forged Bearer token via public endpoint
steps.expected: Request denied (public cannot access provider routes)

evidence.network: All public requests to /provider/* return HTTP 401

assertions.title: Gateway denies unauthenticated or forged callers

steps.action: POST /provider/v1/inference without Authorization header
POST with empty Bearer token (Authorization: Bearer)
POST with Basic auth scheme instead of Bearer
POST with completely forged token format

assertions.title: Provider credentials are injected only on the host

steps.action: Verify gateway-provider.env location on Node B
steps.expected: Credentials stored at /var/lib/go-choir/gateway-provider.env with restricted permissions
steps.observed: File exists at /var/lib/go-choir/gateway-provider.env with rw------- (0600) permissions

steps.action: Verify credential management endpoints are localhost-only
steps.observed: HTTP 403 with error: 'access denied' from public origin

steps.action: Verify credential management works from localhost
steps.expected: 200 OK with credential JSON from localhost
steps.observed: Successfully issued credential from 127.0.0.1 with token and expiry

evidence.network: Public: 403 Forbidden, Localhost: 200 OK with credential

assertions.title: Rate limiting is enforced per sandbox identity

steps.action: Check rate limiter configuration via /health endpoint
steps.expected: Rate limit configured at 60 requests per 60000ms window

steps.action: Send 65 rapid requests with same sandbox credential
steps.expected: First 60 requests accepted, remaining 5 return 429 Too Many Requests

steps.action: Verify rate limit response includes Retry-After header
steps.expected: 429 response includes Retry-After header with seconds remaining
steps.observed: HTTP 429 with Retry-After header and error message including retry window

evidence.network: 65 sequential requests: 60x HTTP 400, 5x HTTP 429 with Retry-After

assertions.title: Unsupported provider or model requests fail closed

steps.action: Check configured provider from health endpoint
Send request with unsupported provider 'openai'
Send request with unsupported provider 'bedrock'
steps.observed: HTTP 400 with error: 'unsupported provider: bedrock' (when zai is configured)

evidence.network: Requests with unsupported providers return HTTP 400 before upstream call

steps.action: Verify error responses are JSON formatted
steps.observed: Errors properly formatted as JSON with 'error' field

steps.action: Check for stack traces in error responses
steps.expected: No stack traces, goroutine dumps, or internal details
steps.observed: No stack traces or internal details in error responses

steps.action: Verify credentials are not leaked in error messages
steps.expected: No api-key, Bearer, token, secret, password, or credential patterns in errors
steps.observed: Errors contain sanitized messages only, no credential leakage

steps.action: Check gateway logs for sanitized error messages
steps.expected: Logs show '(sanitized)' suffix for upstream errors
steps.observed: Journal logs contain: 'zai: status 400 Bad Request (sanitized)'

evidence.network: Error responses properly sanitized with no credential leakage

assertions.title: Stale or replaced sandbox credentials stop working

steps.action: Issue new credential for sandbox 'lifecycle-test'
steps.expected: Credential created with token and expiry
steps.observed: Received credential with RawToken and ExpiresAt fields

steps.action: Test credential is valid before revocation
steps.expected: Request accepted (may fail on body validation but auth passes)
steps.observed: HTTP 400 'invalid request body' (auth passed, body validation failed)

steps.action: Revoke credential via /provider/v1/credentials/revoke
Test same credential after revocation

evidence.network: Before revoke: HTTP 400, After revoke: HTTP 401

frictions.description: JSON request body parsing through SSH/curl escaping was challenging - needed multiple attempts to get proper JSON escaping for request payloads
frictions.resolution: Used single-quoted heredoc style through SSH: ssh node-b 'curl ... -d '''{"field":"value"}''''

frictions.description: Parallel rapid requests for rate limiting test caused temporary SSH connection saturation
frictions.resolution: Reduced parallelism and added delays between request batches

summary: Tested 7 assertions: All 7 passed (VAL-GATEWAY-002 through VAL-GATEWAY-008). Gateway correctly denies public access, enforces authentication, hosts credentials securely, implements per-sandbox rate limiting, rejects unsupported providers, sanitizes errors, and properly handles credential lifecycle.
